title: rhinanthus angustifolius subsp. subalpinus
intro: Rhinanthus angustifolius subsp. subalpinus, also known as the alpine bee orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, characterized by its slender stems and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic and to help reduce fever. The health benefits of Rhinanthus angustifolius subsp. subalpinus include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and its ability to help lower blood pressure. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and to help alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
